prompt_text: Alternatives to LlamaIndex for AI engineers at startups and enterprises building LLM applications
response:
For **AI engineers at startups and enterprises**, the most relevant alternatives to LlamaIndex depend on whether you want a *general LLM app framework*, a *production RAG/search framework*, or a *higher-level enterprise platform*. LlamaIndex itself is strongest for **document ingestion, indexing, and retrieval**, so the main alternatives either go broader than that or specialize around adjacent needs like orchestration, automation, or governance.[4][1]

- **LangChain** — the broadest alternative if you need a flexible framework for many LLM app patterns beyond retrieval, including chains, tools, and agents.[4][2]
- **Haystack** — the closest fit for teams focused on **production RAG** and enterprise search, with modular pipeline control and strong support for search-oriented systems.[1][2]
- **DSPy** — useful if your team wants to optimize LLM pipelines more systematically instead of hand-tuning prompts, especially for research-heavy or performance-sensitive use cases.[2]
- **LangGraph** — a good choice for agentic workflows when you need explicit control over stateful multi-step execution; it is commonly positioned as a production-oriented agent framework in the broader LLM stack.[6]
- **CrewAI** — useful for multi-agent orchestration when you want role-based agent collaboration rather than document retrieval as the core abstraction.[6]
- **Microsoft AutoGen** — another multi-agent option for teams building conversational or collaborative agent systems.[6]
- **Zapier** — best when the goal is *workflow automation* across business tools, not custom infrastructure-heavy LLM engineering.[1]
- **Flowise** — good for low-code prototyping with visual building blocks, especially for teams that want speed over deep code control.[1]
- **Dust** — better for enterprises that want a managed AI agent platform with connectors and governance rather than a framework they assemble themselves.[1]
- **Custom stack** — worth considering if your engineers want maximum control and are comfortable composing embeddings, vector databases, and orchestration pieces themselves.[2]

A practical way to choose:
- If you are building **RAG-heavy products**, start with **Haystack** or **LangChain**, depending on whether you want more search-centric rigor or broader app flexibility.[2][4]
- If you need **agent workflows**, look at **LangGraph**, **CrewAI**, or **AutoGen** rather than a retrieval-first tool.[6]
- If you are an enterprise buyer and want **governance, connectors, and deployment speed**, consider **Dust** or **Zapier** over a framework.[1]
- If you want **minimal code and faster prototyping**, **Flowise** is the most approachable option in the set.[1]

If you want, I can turn this into a **startup vs enterprise comparison table** with recommendations by use case, team size, and technical maturity.
